mechanism_narrative: Robust retail sales growth indicates strong consumer demand, which translates to higher corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward returns are negative or flat (mean return < 0.001) in periods of sustained RSAFS growth, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: A significant drop in the unemployment rate below a critical threshold signals a tightening labor market, which typically precedes wage inflation and strong consumer spending, boosting equity valuations over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative or flat (mean return < 0.001) in more than 50% of instances where UNRATE drops below 5.0%, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is low and housing starts are increasing, it indicates an accommodative monetary policy supporting a recovering housing market, which stimulates broader economic activity and corporate profits, leading to positive equity returns over a 60-day horizon.
regime_hint: post-recession recovery with accommodative monetary policy
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative or flat when FEDFUNDS is low and HOUST is growing, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production signals weakening manufacturing and overall supply-side contraction, which typically leads to lower corporate earnings expectations and negative S&P 500 forward returns, especially during periods of high inflation over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of instances where INDPRO declines significantly during periods of high inflation (CPIAUCSL > 3%), the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth indicates a healthy and expanding economy, which provides a favorable environment for corporate profitability and investor sentiment, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative or flat (mean return < 0.001) in periods where GDPC1 year-over-year growth exceeds 2.0%, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent job creation, as measured by non-farm payrolls, reflects a robust labor market and strong consumer base, which underpins corporate revenues and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the short 5-day term.
regime_hint: any regime with positive job growth
failure_condition: If S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of instances following a monthly PAYEMS increase greater than 100,000, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating inflation, as indicated by CPIAUCSL, erodes purchasing power and corporate profit margins, leading to expectations of tighter monetary policy and thus negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day forward returns are consistently positive (mean return > 0.005) when CPIAUCSL shows accelerating inflation above 4.0%, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts signals a significant slowdown in the construction sector and broader economic demand, which negatively impacts related industries and overall market sentiment, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
regime_hint: economic contraction or housing market downturn
failure_condition: If S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances where HOUST declines by more than 10% month-over-month, the hypothesis is falsified.
